channel.title: GeoPulse. Geopolitics in brief
channel.description: Signal-first briefings on geopolitics, edited by Lavkesh Dwivedi.

item.title: Trump Evacuated As Shots Fired At White House Dinner, Shooter Arrested
item.description: Trump was evacuated from a White House dinner after shots were fired, a shooter was arrested, and several people reported hearing five to eight gunshots. Several people in the crowd said they heard what sounded like five to eight gunshots.

item.title: US-Israel-Iran War Ceasefire News Live Updates: Trump claims Iran sent new peace proposal, says he
item.description: Trump claimed Iran sent a new peace proposal, which he rejected, amid US-Israel-Iran war ceasefire talks. US-Israel-Iran War Ceasefire News Live Updates: Trump claims Iran sent new peace proposal, says he rejected offer The Times of India.

item.title: Trump uninjured after after security incident at White House Correspondents’ Dinner
item.description: Trump was uninjured after a security incident at the White House Correspondents’ Dinner, where guests ducked under tables. The Secret Service and other authorities swarmed the banquet hall as guests ducked under tables by the hundreds.

item.title: Hundreds of Israelis rally as US-Iran peace talks stall
item.description: Hundreds of Israelis rallied in Tel Aviv as US-Iran peace talks stalled, fearing a resumed US-Israeli war on Iran. Crowds of protesters took to the streets of Tel Aviv on Sunday, amid fears the US-Israeli war on Iran will resume.

item.title: Why will China not take the bullet for Iran?
item.description: China will not support Iran due to its own interests, according to Modern Diplomacy. Why will China not take the bullet for Iran?

item.title: Indian police say 47 Maoist rebels surrender
item.description: In Telangana, 47 Maoist rebels surrendered, choosing to "join the mainstream", police said. Almost all key underground leaders have been neutralised, authorities added.

item.title: Trump unharmed after shots heard at White House Correspondents' Dinner
item.description: Shots were heard at the White House Correspondents' Dinner, prompting guests to scramble for cover. Former US President Trump was unharmed in the incident. Guests at the black-tie White House Correspondents' Dinner scrambled to hide under tables.

item.title: Taiwan foreign minister arrives in Eswatini after president's trip blocked
item.description: Taiwan's foreign minister visited Eswatini after President Lai Ching-te's trip was blocked by China. China had pressured African states to deny overflight permission. Taiwan's government has accused China of pressuring three African states to ‌block overflight permission for President Lai Ching-te.

item.title: Viktor Orbán steps back from Parliament after landslide loss, vows to rebuild Hungary’s ’national
item.description: Viktor Orbán stepped back from Hungary's parliament after a landslide loss, vowing to rebuild his party. His caucus will be "radically transformed" and he won't take his seat. Mr. Orbán said his party’s caucus in parliament would be “radically transformed” following the election loss, and that he would not take his seat.

item.title: Trump says cancelled envoys' Pakistan trip, but Iran war not resuming
item.description: Former US President Trump said he cancelled a planned trip to Pakistan by envoys. The move did not signal a resumption of war with Iran, he added. Iran’s Foreign Ministry said Mr.

item.title: Trump evacuated after gunshots heard at White House correspondent’s dinner; shooter held
item.description: Former US President Trump was evacuated after gunshots were heard at the White House Correspondents' Dinner. The shooter was taken into custody following the incident. Trump evacuated after gunshots heard at White House correspondent’s dinner; shooter held.

item.title: U.S. sanctions waiver on Chabahar port ends on April 26, could signal end of 23-year-old
item.description: US officials are discussing a possible transfer of IPGL's stake in Chabahar port to a local Iranian company, temporarily avoiding sanctions after a waiver expires on April 26. The move could signal the end of a 23-year-old agreement.

item.title: Trump safe after being rushed from White House correspondents dinner, shooter in custody
item.description: US President Trump was rushed from the White House correspondents dinner after shots were fired at the Washington Hilton hotel. Trump and all federal officials were safe, and a shooter was apprehended about an hour later.

item.title: Trump evacuated from White House correspondents’ dinner after shots fired
item.description: US President Trump and the Cabinet were unharmed after a shooting at the Washington Hilton hotel during the White House correspondents dinner. Trump was rushed from the event and later reported that a shooter had been apprehended.

item.title: 5 islands to chill out with beaches, trails and village life
item.description: The US military has acknowledged that Iran caused more extensive damage to US military bases than publicly known. The extent of the damage is still unclear, but it has been confirmed that Iran was responsible for the attacks.

item.title: Iran caused more extensive damage to US military bases than publicly known
item.description: with the extent of the damage still unclear. The US military has confirmed that Iran was responsible for the attacks, but details remain scarce. Iran caused more extensive damage to US military bases than publicly known.

item.title: Why Trump cancelled Pakistan talks on Iran; ‘We have all the cards,’ he says
item.description: US President Trump cancelled talks with Pakistan, citing Islamabad's inaction on militant groups. Trump stated, "We have all the cards," indicating US leverage over Pakistan. The move comes amid strained relations over Pakistan's perceived support for Iranian and Afghan militant groups.

item.title: Trump cancels U.S. envoys’ visit to Pakistan for talks; says 'does not mean restart of war'
item.description: US envoys' talks with Pakistan were cancelled by Trump, who said it does not signal a restart of war. Iran's Foreign Minister left Islamabad without meeting the US delegation, and commercial flights resumed from Tehran's airport for the first time in two months.

item.title: Niger And Mali’s Claims Of Terrorism: Growth Of Extremism In The Sahel As Threat To Global Security
item.description: Niger and Mali have accused terrorist groups of escalating violence in the Sahel region, posing a threat to global security. The claims come amid growing instability in the area, with extremist groups expanding their reach.

item.title: Pakistan Emerges As Key Middle Power Amid Shifting Global Security Order
item.description: Pakistan is emerging as a key middle power in the shifting global security order. The country's strategic location and growing diplomatic engagements have elevated its status. Analysts see Pakistan playing a crucial role in regional dynamics, balancing relations with major powers like China, Russia, and the US.
